words.meaning_en: A triangular landform created by sediment deposited where a river meets the sea
words.example_sentence: The Sundarbans is the world's largest river delta.
words.fill_blank: The Ganga-Brahmaputra ______ is one of the most fertile regions in the world.

words.meaning_en: A piece of land almost entirely surrounded by water but connected to a larger landmass
words.example_sentence: India is a peninsula as it is surrounded by sea on three sides.
words.fill_blank: The Deccan Plateau forms much of the Indian ______.

words.meaning_en: The angular distance of a place north or south of the Earth's equator, measured in degrees
words.example_sentence: New Delhi is located at approximately 28° north latitude.
words.fill_blank: The Tropic of Cancer lies at 23.5° north ______.

words.meaning_en: A partially enclosed coastal area where a river meets the sea and freshwater mixes with saltwater
words.example_sentence: Many migratory birds nest in the estuary during winter months.
words.fill_blank: The ______ is an important habitat for fish and water birds.

words.meaning_en: The seasonal reversal of wind direction that brings heavy rainfall to South Asia
words.example_sentence: Farmers in India depend on the monsoon for irrigating their crops.
words.fill_blank: The southwest ______ arrives in India around June every year.

words.meaning_en: A river or stream that flows into and joins a larger river
words.example_sentence: The Yamuna is a major tributary of the Ganga.
words.fill_blank: The Chambal is a ______ of the Yamuna river.
